labels.name: claim_block
labels.description: Returns the step-by-step instructions for claiming a Times Rare block. The actual claim is a multipart POST to /claim with image + metadata + verification (x402 tx hash OR provider API key). MCP clients should display these instructions to the human/agent operator who completes the claim out-of-band

labels.name: get_block
labels.description: Read a specific block by its share_token (the permanent URL slug at /b/<share_token>). Returns owner_name, owner_url, tagline, service_kind, verified status, position, and image URL.

labels.name: submit_demand_survey
labels.description: Submit an anonymous response to the Times Rare demand survey (used to research agent-economy ad pricing + feature priorities). Rate-limited 3 responses per IP per hour. No identity stored.

labels.name: get_survey_results
labels.description: Read the aggregate Times Rare demand-survey results. Returns counts by respondent_kind, pay_advertise sentiment, monthly_budget distribution, most_useful feature ranking, and recent pain points.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "streamable-http" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_services, get_block · Private data: claim_block · Public sink: claim_block, submit_demand_survey.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_services, get_block · Private data: claim_block · Public sink: claim_block, submit_demand_survey

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
